name: neat
short: Remove clutter from Kubernetes manifests to make them more readable
description: neat trims away fields that Kubernetes adds to resources automatically (like managedFields, status, creationTimestamp) so you can focus on the meaningful parts of a manifest.

name: stern
short: Multi pod and container log tailing
description: Stern tails logs from multiple pods and containers simultaneously, color-coding output by pod name. Supports glob patterns, regex filtering, and timestamps.

name: tree
short: Show a tree of object hierarchies through ownerReferences
description: tree visualises the ownership chain of Kubernetes objects via ownerReferences, letting you see how Deployments relate to ReplicaSets and Pods in a single command.

name: images
short: Show container images used in the cluster
description: images lists all container images running across pods in a namespace, including init containers, with their pull policy and digest.

name: whoami
short: Show the subject currently authenticated as
description: whoami calls the Kubernetes TokenReview API to display your current username and group memberships — useful for debugging RBAC issues.

name: df-pv
description: df-pv reports disk usage statistics (capacity, used, available, percent used) for PersistentVolumeClaims in your cluster, similar to the Unix df command.

name: outdated
short: Finds outdated container images running in a cluster
description: outdated scans all pods in a namespace and checks whether their container images are using a pinned digest or a mutable tag, helping identify images that may be out of date.
